provisions.content: These By-laws may be cited as the Makete District Council (Development Levy ) By-laws, 2002 and shall operate within the whole area of jurisdiction of the Makete District Council .
These By-laws shall come into operation on the day they are published in the Gazette .

provisions.title: Definitions
provisions.content: In these By-laws unless the context requires otherwise:— " Council " means the Makete District Council " Councilor " means a member of the Council as defined by The Local Government (District Authorities) Act, 1982. " Education fund " means funds available for education matters. " Employee " means a person employed by the Government, its institutions or any self-operating non-governmental institution within Makete District. " Levy " means the annual rate designated as "Development levy" made by the Council and payable under the provision of these By-laws and in accordance with the Local Government Finances Act. [No.9 of 1982] " Resident " means any person who resides in the area of Jurisdiction of the Council . " Trader " means any person who has been issued a trading licence and operates a legal business within Makete District.

With effect from 1 st day of January, 2002 all re s dents individual of the Council who is liable to pay levy shall pay to the Council such levy according to the categories and the rates stipulated in schedule B of these By-laws
The sum of one thousand shillings shall be deducted upon each rate collected for education matters.
The thirty first day of July of every year shall be the last day upon which any person Iiable to pay the levy shall have paid it to the Council .

Where after the thirty first day of July of every year, a person liable to pay the levy has not paid it, shall in addition to the levy, pay a penalty equal to fifty percentum of the levy payable by him.

The Makete District Council Development Levy (Amendment) By-laws 1998 are hereby repealed. Schedule Part "A"Category Monthly Income Part "B" Rate per Year TShs. 0-46,000/= 5,000/= TShs. 46,001-65,000/= 6.000/= TShs 65,001-75,000/= 7,000/= TShs. 75,001-90,000/= 8,000/= TShs. 90,001-100,000/= 9,000/= TShs. 100,001-110,000/= 10,000/= TShs. 110,001-130,000/= 11,000/= TShs. 130,001-and over 16,000/= 2. Trader: (a) Retailers, Pharmacy Petrol station and Guest Houses 10,000/= (b) Groceries, Bar and other Business 8,000/= 3. Councilors 8,000/= 4. Other Residents 4,500/= History of this document 26 July 2002 this version Published in Gazette of the United Republic of Tanzania 30 Commenced 22 March 2002 Assented to Related documents More documents like this one [] [] [] [] ▲ To the top > Contact us Visit our Twitter/X profile Visit our Linked In profile Visit our YouTube channel About TanzLII TanzLII is operated by the Judiciary of Tanzania and the Office of the Attorney General, Chief Parliamentary Draftsman, and publishes the law of Tanzania for free online access for all.
